item.title: Old Family Photos?
item.content:encoded:
Wouldn't someone have thought to test it's effects with regard to time as well as space? A photo of grandma and grandpa's wedding maybe?

That seems like a huge oversight.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Have we considered cross SCP testing with SCP-786? this would allow easy access to anyone (or anything) under 1.14 m tall.

It would be hazerdous for real world applications (except for maybe espionage) but for abstract testing (or making a containment site within a painting) it would be ideal.

meaning we could visit SCP-085 if we really wanted, and allow you to get rid of that cumbersome 'small frame' bit in tests 6 and 7, because anyone able to fit through 786 could be used for testing. (also would allow for more extensive testing, which I would love.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What would if you inserted a frame of a picture that had experienced motion blur from the camera moving?

Test: Long term exposure shot with the camera rotating 90 degrees.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Violently torn apart by the gravity implied, and required, to make such a floor plan conceivable."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"I've got an idea."
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perhaps the lazy expunger employed by Yoric has been transferred to Site-██…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the hint of including a partial word, but I can't imagine why anyone would do that. It doesn't fit the whole idea of expunging data.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You have two tests listed as test 5 this should be corrected

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Test four is exactly what I would have done upon finding that out.
Gotta wonder how such an object would feel about smashed up particles too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I took the liberty of correcting the spelling of "Relativity". I do like this version of the experimental note, though. "D-246 was vio[DATA EXPUNGED BY ORDER OF O5 COMMAND]." It's fun to try to imagine what fate was so horrible that the Overseers don't even want people reading about it…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This. Writing is solid, idea is not. Downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought about putting Cassy in the frame, but couldn't exactly decide what would happen to her… I guess it would be possible to send someone through the frame to hang out with her on her level. Fixing the frame size, did not think of that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Another SCP with endless possibilities. The inclusion of fantasy landscapes really did it for me, though I agree with above on the ability to climb through the frame.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Another thought: If we put something into a reproduction of a drawing or painting, would it appear in every other copy of that? What happens if we stick something in a non-real place and then remove the picture from the frame? Does it stay animate or does it freeze?

Building up to: would we be able to free Cassy?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've used metric my whole life, and yeah, 25x20 cms is MAYBE big enough for a newborn, but not a grown adult, even if she's on the small side.

Suggestion: Remotely controlled vehicle/robot with a camera. Would the signal travel back through the portal, I wonder?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait. 25cm x 20cm is roughly 8"x10" for us Imperial-measurements readers. There is no way anyone could crawl through that; I don't care how small they are. Reach through it, yeah; stick a camera through it, yeah; but not crawl through it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seeing this after a bit of rewriting, I now *really really* want to know what happened to the poor schlub walking up the endless stairs…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It feels too useful to me. Portal to anywhere, woo hoo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, when I was reading this, I thought it was a reponse to Bright's "reverse it" challenge. It's not a bad article; it's just not grabbing me. I'm going to refrain from voting right now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"It should be noted that D-246 did not [DATA EXPUNGED] despite the [DATA EXPUNGED]"

You could probably get rid of this. This article is still pretty good, especially for a first try. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Affect is the verb and effect the noun, changed that for you :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Minor point, but most radiation scientists that I know don't refer to beta radiation as waves. Yes, a quantum-mechanical interpretation of beta-particles can be thought of as a wave, but it's normally not done.

Aside from that, a fairly good first attempt, although I feel it needs a bit more of a hook.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Would not having the D-class stay in the room for the test reel things in a bit? I'll replace it with the Art tests I'm working on.
The idea wasn't that they had a base on Mars, it was just "We could do this, but probably won't because the hole's too small and NASA found something crazy up there." I'll try to make it more clear.
How do I make those fancy collapsible test logs?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's uh, certainly interesting. A couple of years ago, i probably would have upvoted it. But as it stands now… the last experiment and the note at the end just strikes me as too much 'lolfoundation.' "We have a base on mars too, teehee! aren't we awesome?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Paintings could be interesting, especially Escher and Dali. I'll work on that. I totally forgot the name, Thanks for mentioning (and teaching me how to name it).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The execution is very, very good for a first attempt.

The effect, however, is very similar to the one SCP-105 have.

You should add a name to this, by editing SCP Series page.

If you decide to change it to make it less similar to 105, allowing the frame to work on paintings could be a nice twist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! I've been sitting here F5'ing since I posted it waiting for some form of validation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, I think it's good. It wouldn't be too remarkable if it wasn't for that bit about zooming. +1

item.title: Oh boy..
item.content:encoded:
Well, my first article. Internet, I bear my soul to thee.
Don't hold back, any feedback is welcomed, even if it's just "You suck and I hate you so hard that I want you to die until you're dead."
